consistency_findings.finding: The Issue 162 historical_replay_attempt_ledger.yaml is an early ACTIVE checkpoint and does not contain the final three accepted fully instrumented runs or the reduced confirmation documented by the later final formal report.
consistency_findings.adjudication: There is no Issue 162 final machine Gate, so this is not a final-Gate-versus-ledger conflict. The higher-priority final formal report is supported independently by four tracked ACCEPTED processed attempt_result.json files with exact affected identities and matching Successor Oracle results. The checkpoint remains unchanged and the limitation remains visible.

consistency_findings.finding: The prior current narrative predates the closure of N1, C1, R1, W1, and B1 and still presents several closed questions as future priorities.
consistency_findings.adjudication: Narrative V7 and current-state files replace only the current interpretation; no historical report or Gate is modified.

consistency_findings.finding: P0 Phase A.2 has measurement and re-entry Gates, while P2 and P3 predate the later per-campaign final-Gate convention and rely on formal reports plus processed matrices.
consistency_findings.adjudication: The formal reports and tracked 18-row and 24-row matrices agree. P3's five overall Route UNKNOWN results remain UNKNOWN and are not counted as PASS.

MG1.name: Unified Problem Existence
MG1.exact_claim: Route conformance, command freshness, and lifecycle successor progression are distinct but coupled runtime-consistency obligations for route-replacing authority transitions.
MG1.prohibited_stronger_claim: Ordinary handoffs generally fail or the abstraction covers every PX4 authority transition.
MG1.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: A bounded Family A method preregistration may be considered if the remaining entry clauses permit it.

MG2.name: Oracle Incremental Value
MG2.exact_claim: Route, Freshness, Successor, and Evidence Gates distinguish lifecycle dead ends, pre-revocation exposure, post-revocation subject residue, route identity, and inadmissible evidence that mode-only or physical-only checks cannot attribute.
MG2.prohibited_stronger_claim: Every Oracle clause is complete in every study or every Oracle result is a PASS.
MG2.consequence_for_final_disposition: Oracle incremental value satisfies a core Motivation requirement.
MG2.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: The frozen Family A Oracles may serve as preregistered evaluation instruments.

MG3.name: Normal Baseline Credibility
MG3.exact_claim: Normal entry, release, fallback, clean re-entry, matched mechanism, and legal successor-chain behavior have evidence-complete bounded baselines on the frozen Family A stack.
MG3.prohibited_stronger_claim: P5's 70 Route PASS sides establish a general safety rate or R1 session rollover readiness.
MG3.consequence_for_final_disposition: The current findings are interpretable against credible normal baselines.
MG3.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: Family A bounded seeds are admissible; the R1 gap must not be imported.

MG4.name: Historical Benchmark Feasibility
MG4.exact_claim: Issue 162 supplies an exact-version, public-trigger historical benchmark with 3/3 accepted fully instrumented reproductions, 1/1 reduced-observation confirmation, a legal baseline, and current constructor prevention.
MG4.prohibited_stronger_claim: Issue 162 is executable on the current library or the reduced confirmation is a separate defect.
MG4.consequence_for_final_disposition: Known-benchmark detection is feasible for later bounded method evaluation.
MG4.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: A later preregistration may include this historical benchmark without reopening its campaign.

MG5.name: Current Natural Evidence
MG5.exact_claim: The current natural stale-subject Route event was observed once in the frozen pilot and re-observed in two accepted late-phase N1 runs.
MG5.prohibited_stronger_claim: The event has a stable occurrence rate, is phase-independent, has a proved root cause, or carries old external allocator or writer authority.
MG5.consequence_for_final_disposition: Current natural evidence exists, but the Motivation result must remain conditional.
MG5.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: Later work may preregister bounded rediscovery and reproduction metrics, not assume deterministic reproduction.

MG6.name: Partial Failure and Freshness Relevance
MG6.exact_claim: Health, setpoint, and process decoupling create measurable differences in route retention, fallback, command age, command lineage, and bounded physical context.
MG6.prohibited_stronger_claim: Freshness EXPOSURE is automatically a defect or the incomplete Attitude cell estimates a general outcome.
MG6.consequence_for_final_disposition: Freshness and partial-failure relevance are empirically supported.
MG6.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: Family A seed selection may include only the already supported channel semantics under a new cap.

MG7.name: Concurrency and Re-entry Readiness
MG7.exact_claim: C1 provides a bounded public-event seed grammar and linearization invariant, and P0 supplies clean re-entry evidence; session-rollover readiness remains unmeasured.
MG7.prohibited_stronger_claim: All authority-event concurrency is linearizable or session rollover passed.
MG7.consequence_for_final_disposition: This limits full readiness but does not erase the bounded Family A grammar.
MG7.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: A Family A preregistration must exclude R1's unfinished session-rollover scope.

MG8.name: Real Workload Added Value
MG8.exact_claim: The source audit classifies workload interfaces and task-versus-route transitions, but no accepted runtime source trace exists to determine added route, lifecycle, timing, or context value.
MG8.prohibited_stronger_claim: The real workload adds no value, has validated runtime value, or should enter the core empirical contribution.
MG8.consequence_for_final_disposition: A full Motivation PASS is unavailable.
MG8.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: No real-workload or Native Adapter campaign is authorized.

MG9.name: Cross-Depth / Family B Generality
MG9.exact_claim: The locked source contains two true registered controller routes and a bounded static reference contract, but no accepted combined build or runtime graph-replacement evidence exists.
MG9.prohibited_stronger_claim: Family B runtime generality, route installation, writer exclusivity, restoration, or controller safety is validated.
MG9.consequence_for_final_disposition: A full cross-family Motivation PASS is unavailable; Family B remains future work.
MG9.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: No Family B runtime or direct-actuator work is authorized.

MG10.name: Attribution and Method-Entry Readiness
MG10.exact_claim: Version, mechanism, instrumentation, clock, lineage, acceptance, rejection, seed, and Oracle disciplines are sufficient to preregister a bounded Family A method evaluation.
MG10.prohibited_stronger_claim: State-aware search gain or full fuzzing effectiveness has already been demonstrated.
MG10.consequence_for_final_disposition: Method entry is conditionally supported for Family A only.
MG10.consequence_for_next_stage_authorization: Only an independent Family A Fuzzer v0 preregistration is authorized; no execution starts here.

claims: Ordinary single-event Family A handoffs were route-conforming within the frozen bounded baselines.
Route, Freshness, Successor, and Evidence Gates provide distinct attribution value.
Issue 162 is a reproducible historical lifecycle benchmark and is prevented by the current library's constructor guard.
The current stale-subject event was re-observed but is phase-dependent.
Freshness exposure and health/setpoint decoupling are relevant to route and command-lineage validation.
C1 supports the frozen bounded event-pair grammar.
Family A is the completed formal empirical scope of the Motivation Study.
Family B static mechanism evidence exists and its runtime generality remains future work.
prohibited_claims: P5 proves general or population-level safety.
Freshness EXPOSURE is equivalent to a software defect.
The current natural event has a stable rate, phase-independent trigger, or proved root cause.
Issue 162 is an executable current-version defect or its Route Oracle NOT_APPLICABLE result is PASS.
R1 session rollover passed or failed as a SUT result.
W1 proves a real workload has no added value or validates runtime added value.
B1 validates Family B runtime graph replacement, writer lineage, or restoration.
Dynamic External Mode is safer than Offboard.
State-aware search gain or full fuzzing effectiveness is established.
A safety stop, environment failure, campaign configuration failure, UNKNOWN, or NOT_APPLICABLE is a PASS or SUT failure.
limitations: The evidence is bounded to frozen SITL revisions, vehicles, configurations, Oracles, thresholds, and accepted windows.
P3 retains five overall Route UNKNOWN results in accepted channel-behavior cases.
Freshness F2 is measurement-insufficient at one accepted run of three planned.
N1 supports phase-dependent re-observation but not stable reproduction.
C1 lacks the A/near accepted slot and does not cover arbitrary scheduler interleavings.
R1 has zero accepted session-rollover runs.
W1 has zero accepted source traces and no replay evidence.
B1 has no accepted combined build or runtime evidence.
Issue 162's historical checkpoint ledger is not a final aggregate ledger; the final report and accepted processed records carry the final accounting.
No state-aware or random search comparison has run.
